scope_note: This registry does NOT screen, score, or select features -- it only records what each candidate IS, where it comes from, and what constraints apply, so Phase 7D's validators and any future screening step have a single source of truth. Categorical/identifier fields that define the interaction graph itself (sender_id, receiver_id, interaction_type, status_before/status_after, agent_id, call_id, message_id, part_ids, artifact_ids) are deliberately NOT registered here as candidate features -- they define the adjacency/topology `graph_source: interaction_events` refers to, not a value attached to a node/edge/session. This registry covers derived scalar/dict VALUES computed on top of that topology.

ground_truth_fields.reason: attack/difficulty-adjacent ground truth, never eligible raw material -- see raw_metadata_schema.json's excluded_ground_truth_fields.

dataset_provenance_fields.reason: describe how a task was generated, not what happened during execution -- see raw_metadata_schema.json's out_of_scope_dataset_provenance_fields.

graph_topology_fields.reason: define the graph's structure/identifiers/diagnostic masks, not a scalar or vector feature value attached to it. active_agent_mask/adjacency_signature specifically remain the Step 6.5D LightGAE-input-READINESS diagnostic, not a trainable feature.

non_numeric_status_fields.reason: categorical session outcome fields already used by Step 6.5D's behavioral validation (branch_exact_match etc.) -- diagnostic, not proposed here as a LightGAE input candidate.

leakage_risk.note: no ground-truth field involved; risk here is the confound, not leakage.
confound, not leakage.

features.formula: count of distinct (sender_id, receiver_id) pairs in the session
count of distinct (sender_id, receiver_id) pairs seen more than once in the session
features.notes: IDENTICAL formula to graph_features.parallel_edge_count -- computed independently by formal_workload_mock_run.py (Step 6.5D) and feature_generators/graph_features.py (Step 7B). Kept as two registry entries because they come from two independently-tested code paths, but Phase 7D/screening should treat these as ONE feature, not two.

features.formula: count of events where status_before != status_after

leakage_risk.note: revision-volume proxy at the artifact level -- correlated with revision_count by construction, not by attack signal.
size only, never Part.content itself.

features.formula: count of revision_request-type messages in the session

leakage_risk.note: confound, not leakage -- see Dataset Card 'hard-tier/budget_revision confound'.

features.formula: count of clarification_request-type messages in the session

leakage_risk.note: near-zero once the Ollama model is warm -- may carry little signal beyond the first call of a run, worth screening for near-constant variance later.

mock value is a synthetic-but-real DeterministicClock diff, not a real elapsed-time measurement -- must be partitioned by timing_source before any cross-session comparison, never mixed with ollama_runtime values.

features.notes: IDENTICAL formula to session_features.call_count -- same duplication situation as repeated_pair_count/parallel_edge_count.

features.formula: count of calls where llm_called is True

leakage_risk.note: always 0 under deterministic_mock (mock_runner.py never sets llm_called=True) -- a degenerate constant, not a missing value, until an ollama_runtime session exists.

features.formula: output_token_count / input_token_count (None if input_token_count is 0 or None)
predecessor_output_token_count / input_token_count (None if no resolvable predecessor or input_token_count is 0/None)

leakage_risk.note: predecessor resolution breaks fan-in ties by latest call_end_timestamp then call_id -- deterministic, but an ambiguous multi-producer case picks only one predecessor; worth re-checking once real multi-agent fan-in sessions exist.
same predecessor-resolution caveat as predecessor_output_ratio.

features.formula: passthrough of the raw node field

leakage_risk.note: identical value to the Phase 7A raw node field -- carried through for convenient co-access with the other timing_features, not an independent measurement.

features.formula: eval_count / (eval_duration / 1e9) (None if eval_duration is 0/None or eval_count is None)

leakage_risk.note: captures load_duration plus unattributed overhead -- interpretability is weaker than the other timing fields, worth revisiting once real ollama_runtime values exist.
not a scalar -- a per-agent map. A future flattening step (one column per agent x {fan_in,fan_out}) is a screening-stage decision, not made here.

features.formula: unique_directed_pair_count / (n * (n-1)) where n = distinct active agents; 0.0 if n < 2
count of distinct (sender_id, receiver_id) pairs seen more than once
features.notes: IDENTICAL formula to repeated_pair_count -- see that entry's notes.

features.formula: longest request_message_id reply chain in the session

leakage_risk.note: closest available analogue to the spec's 'path_depth' -- this Message model has no separate DAG-depth field.

features.formula: longest parent_artifact_ids chain in the session
population stdev / mean of wall_clock_latency_ms across the session's calls (None if < 2 values or mean == 0)
population stdev / mean of eval_count across the session's calls (None if < 2 values or mean == 0)
population stdev / mean of per-message part_ids count across the session (None if < 2 values or mean == 0)
population stdev / mean of per-artifact total size_bytes across the session (None if < 2 values or mean == 0)

leakage_risk.note: size only, never Part.content.

features.formula: max(eval_count) / mean(eval_count) across the session's calls (None if no values or mean == 0)

features.notes: IDENTICAL formula to total_call_count -- see that entry's notes.

source_fields: any node-level numeric metric (e.g. wall_clock_latency_ms, eval_count)

leakage_risk.note: normal_statistics MUST be fit on the train split's NORMAL sessions only (dataset_policy.json's split-unit discipline) -- fitting on validation/test or on attack sessions would leak split/label information into every zscore feature.

features.notes: Formula-only entry -- normalization_features.py never fits normal_statistics itself (Step 7B). enabled=false until a train-split-only fit exists (Step 8+).

leakage_risk.note: REQUIRED for stratified analysis (mock vs. ollama_runtime) -- must never be silently mixed into a model input alongside timing-derived features, only used to partition them.
constant (llama3.2) across this entire workload -- see Dataset Card 'one local model' limitation; would only become discriminative once a second model is ever run.
fixed for this project -- no variance to key on.
useful for cross-prompt-version stratified analysis, never as a direct model input.
